title: Planes in 3D
summary: Normal vector equation of a plane, scalar form, distance from point to plane and from origin, angle between planes, line-plane intersection, three-point plane, parametric form.
content_html:
3.2 Equation of a Plane

Normal vector form: A plane through point A with normal n:
n·(r−a) = 0  →  n₁x + n₂y + n₃z = d
where d = n₁a₁ + n₂a₂ + n₃a₃ = n·a
Example: Plane through A(1,2,3) with normal n=(2,−1,4):
2(x−1)−1(y−2)+4(z−3) = 0
→ 2x − y + 4z = 12

3.2.1 Distance from Origin to Plane

d = |D| / |n|  where plane is n·r = D

3.2.2 Distance from Point P to Plane

d = |n·p − D| / |n|  where plane is n·r = D
Example: Distance from P(1,0,2) to plane 2x−y+4z=12:
d = |2(1)−1(0)+4(2)−12| / √(4+1+16) = |2+8−12| / √21 = 2/√21 ≈ 0.436

3.2.3 Angle Between Two Planes

cos θ = |n₁·n₂| / (|n₁||n₂|)
Two planes are parallel if n₁ = λn₂. They are perpendicular if n₁·n₂ = 0.

3.2.4 Plane Through Three Points

Given A, B, C on the plane: n = AB×AC (normal = cross product of two edge vectors)

Example: Plane through A(1,0,0), B(0,2,0), C(0,0,3):
AB = (−1,2,0), AC = (−1,0,3)
n = AB×AC = (6,3,2)
Equation: 6x + 3y + 2z = 6·1 = 6

3.2.5 Intersection of Line and Plane

Substitute parametric line r = a+td into plane equation, solve for t, then find point.

3.2.6 Parametric Equation of a Plane

r = a + su + tv
where u and v are two non-parallel vectors in the plane (s,t ∈ ℝ)

exercises.title: Normal vector role
exercises.prompt: The normal vector n to a plane is:
options_json: Perpendicular to every vector in the plane
A direction vector in the plane
exercises.answer: Perpendicular to every vector in the plane

exercises.title: Parallel planes
options_json: Their normals are scalar multiples n₁ = λn₂
exercises.answer: Their normals are scalar multiples n₁ = λn₂

exercises.prompt: Normal to plane through A, B, C is:

exercises.title: Line-plane intersection
exercises.prompt: To find where line r=a+td meets plane n·r=D:
options_json: Substitute into plane equation to get t = (D−n·a)/(n·d)
Use cross product of n and d
exercises.answer: Substitute into plane equation to get t = (D−n·a)/(n·d)

exercises.title: Parametric plane
exercises.prompt: Parametric form of a plane is r = a + su + tv where:
options_json: u and v are two non-parallel vectors lying in the plane
u and v are normal vectors
s and t are fixed constants
exercises.answer: u and v are two non-parallel vectors lying in the plane
